name: Chicken Barley Soup (Low Cal)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place chicken, water, leaves from celery and small onion in a large saucepan. Reserve celery stalks.
2. Cover saucepan and bring to a boil; reduce heat and simmer 1 1/2 hours until chicken is tender. Remove chicken. Strain broth into bowl; chill until fat sets on top. Remove fat.
3. Remove skin and bones from chicken, discard. Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces, set aside.
4. Return broth to saucepan. Chop reserved celery stalks, add to broth with chopped onion, carrot, parsley, barley, lemon juice, seasonings. Cover and simmer 20 minutes.
5. Add fresh green beans and chicken; continue cooking 15 minutes or until beans are tender. Each serving contains 1 1/2 cups.
Serving size: 1 1/2 cups
Nutritional information per serving: 11 g carbohydrate, 14 g protein, 5 g fat, 145 calories (2 Protein Choices, 1 fruit & veg. choice)
Source: Choice Cooking, c. 1986 Canadian Diabetes Association, tested Sept 93 with substitutions. From the files of Al Rice, North Pole, Alaska, Feb 1994
